Budget Gap
The portfolio's transactional SAP estate (Concur, Ariba P2P) is healthy across all four customers, but two strategic Commerce Cloud contracts totaling $1.45M and the BTP platform foundation are dormant — that is where executive attention is needed.
Exposure Areas
SAP Commerce Cloud at Cardinal Health and Medtronic — combined $1.45M contract value, both consuming at 4-8% utilization with no credible recovery path in remaining 7 months
SAP BTP Enterprise Agreement at AbbVie (zero across 5 sub-areas) and Medtronic (fragmented 518% in one area, zero in five) — the platform foundation is either dormant or ungoverned, blocking AI, Build, and Integration value at both customers
Watchlist Screening at zero consumption at Medtronic — trade compliance exposure for a global medical device manufacturer, not a cost question
Ariba Buying & Invoicing at Abbott Laboratories (43% contract utilization) — material outlier versus peer healthcare customers running 99-100% utilization
Cloud for Customer and SAP Incentive Management at Abbott — combined CX renewal risk with $384K of contract value at 36-58% utilization
Expansion Opportunities
SAP Integration Suite at Abbott Laboratories at 103% contract utilization — expansion conversation appropriate now, paired with HANA Cloud and Data Intelligence Cloud workload review for FY27
Integrated Business Planning at Abbott Laboratories at 100% utilization — model deployment that can anchor a supply chain AI / Joule expansion conversation
Concur module suite across AbbVie, Cardinal, and Medtronic running 100-131% attainment — model adoption that can anchor a Preferred Success-led adoption expansion into underperforming Ariba modules
Ariba Supplier Risk and Supplier Lifecycle activation across AbbVie, Cardinal, and Medtronic — three customers with the same governance gap and a single repeatable enablement motion
Attainment ≥ 90% on track 75–89% watch < 75% action needed
